documentation-policy.purpose: Keep project documentation useful, traceable, and free from competing sources of truth.

principles.rule: Documentation is part of design, not an appendix after implementation.
One document should have one primary job.
One important topic should have one primary source of truth.
Location carries meaning; use the docs taxonomy before creating files.
Metadata should stay lightweight and maintained.
Links should express real relationships, not decoration.

taxonomy.category: System shape, boundaries, dependency direction, and high-level structure.
Long-lived engineering decisions with context, options, rationale, and consequences.
Specific business capabilities, components, domains, packages, or bounded areas.
How the team builds, reviews, tests, and ships work.
Lookup-oriented schemas, glossary, contracts, and naming rules.

document-status.status: Still being shaped and not yet authoritative.
Kept for history, not current guidance.

rules.rule: Do not create root-level markdown files unless the project entry point requires it.
Use docs/README.md to choose the target category.
Use folder README files as category hubs.
New non-root notes should include a parent relationship.
If a document replaces another, mark the older document superseded and link the replacement.
Do not duplicate long source-of-truth content into another document.
